title: Poor maternal nutrition programmes a pro-atherosclerotic phenotype in ApoE-/- mice.
ehr_evidence: None. The study uses an experimental animal model (ApoE− / − mice).
summary: The study investigates the effects of maternal low-protein (LP) diet during pregnancy and lactation on the development of atherosclerosis in ApoE− / − mouse offspring.
key_findings: LP offspring showed significantly greater plaque area (P < 0.05)
significantly higher indices of DNA damage
increased expression of HMG-CoA reductase and LDL receptor in the liver
